summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened by passionately affirming his belief in miracles, both in the Bible and in the modern day, declaring that Christianity itself is founded on the miraculous. He then transitioned into a teaching from John 4, focusing on Jesus's encounter with the Samaritan woman at the well. Pastor Travis provided historical context, explaining the deep animosity between Jews and Samaritans, and highlighted Jesus as a 'bridge builder' who tore down walls of division—between people, races, and ultimately between God and humanity through the cross. He illustrated this further with the story of Jesus healing a leper from Luke 5, emphasizing that Jesus is 'willing' to touch, heal, and embrace those society rejects. The core of the message explored the 'living water' Jesus offers, which alone can satisfy the deep spiritual thirst that worldly pursuits cannot. Pastor Travis concluded by explaining that true worship is not about a specific place or religion but about a personal relationship with Jesus Christ, the Messiah. He ended with an altar call, inviting those present to surrender their lives to Jesus, the ultimate miracle.
mainPoints: Jesus is a bridge builder who breaks down walls of division and becomes the bridge between God and humanity.
The world cannot satisfy our deepest spiritual thirst; only a relationship with Jesus, the living water, can.
True worship is not about a place, religion, or ritual; it is about a personal relationship with Jesus Christ.

versesMentioned.text: So he left Judea and went back once more to Galilee. Now he had to go through Samaria. So he came to a town in Samaria called Sychar, near the plot of ground Jacob had given to his son Joseph. Jacob’s well was there, and Jesus, tired as he was from the journey, sat down by the well. It was about noon. When a Samaritan woman came to draw water, Jesus said to her, 'Will you give me a drink?' (His disciples had gone into the town to buy food.) The Samaritan woman said to him, 'You are a Jew and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink?' (For Jews do not associate with Samaritans.)

After this I looked, and there before me was a great multitude that no one could count, from every nation, tribe, people and language, standing before the throne and before the Lamb. They were wearing white robes and were holding palm branches in their hands. And they cried out in a loud voice: 'Salvation belongs to our God, who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb.'
For there is one God and one mediator between God and mankind, the man Christ Jesus, who gave himself as a ransom for all people. This has now been witnessed to at the proper time.

While Jesus was in one of the towns, a man came along who was covered with leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he fell with his face to the ground and begged him, 'Lord, if you are willing, you can make me clean.' Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man. 'I am willing,' he said. 'Be clean!' And immediately the leprosy left him.
Jesus answered her, 'If you knew the gift of God and who it is that asks you for a drink, you would have asked him and he would have given you living water.'

Jesus answered, 'Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again, but whoever drinks the water I give them will never thirst. Indeed, the water I give them will become in them a spring of water welling up to eternal life.'
Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled.

Jesus declared, 'Believe me, a time is coming when you will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem. You Samaritans worship what you do not know; we worship what we do know, for salvation is from the Jews. Yet a time is coming and has now come when the true worshipers will worship the Father in the Spirit and in truth, for they are the kind of worshipers the Father seeks. God is spirit, and his worshipers must worship in the Spirit and in truth.'
Then Jesus declared, 'I, the one speaking to you—I am he.'
Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God—this is your true and proper worship.

Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you. For everyone who asks receives; the one who seeks finds; and to the one who knocks, the door will be opened.

Monday.scripture: John 4:10 - 'If you knew the gift of God and who it is that asks you for a drink, you would have asked him and he would have given you living water.'
Monday.focus: Recognizing the gift of God in Jesus.
Monday.action: Identify one area of your life where you've been seeking satisfaction in something other than God. Acknowledge it in prayer.
Monday.prayer: Lord, open my eyes to see You as the ultimate gift. Help me to ask You for the living water that truly satisfies.

Tuesday.scripture: Luke 5:13 - 'Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man. 'I am willing,' he said. 'Be clean!''
Tuesday.focus: The willingness of Jesus to heal and restore.
Tuesday.action: Reflect on an area where you feel 'unclean' or rejected. In a moment of quiet, receive the truth that Jesus is willing to touch and heal that area.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, thank You for being willing. I bring my brokenness to You today. Touch me and make me clean.

Wednesday.scripture: John 4:13-14 - 'Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again, but whoever drinks the water I give them will never thirst.'
Wednesday.action: When you feel a craving or thirst today (literal or metaphorical), pause and take a moment to pray, asking Jesus to fill you instead.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, You are the living water. I choose to drink from You today. Satisfy the deep thirst of my soul.

Thursday.scripture: John 4:23-24 - 'True worshipers will worship the Father in the Spirit and in truth... God is spirit, and his worshipers must worship in the Spirit and in truth.'
Thursday.focus: Worship as a lifestyle, not a location.
Thursday.action: Offer a simple act of your day (e.g., your work, a chore, a kind word) as an act of worship to God.
Thursday.prayer: Father, I offer my life as worship today. Help me to honor You in spirit and in truth in everything I do.

Friday.scripture: Romans 12:1 - 'Offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God—this is your true and proper worship.'
Friday.action: Intentionally lay down one personal preference or desire today as a 'living sacrifice' to serve or bless someone else.
Friday.prayer: God, in view of Your mercy, I offer myself to You. Use my life as a sacrifice that pleases You today.

Saturday.scripture: Matthew 7:7 - 'Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you.'
Saturday.focus: The promise for those who pursue God.
Saturday.action: Spend a few minutes in prayer simply asking, seeking, and knocking. Be specific about your desire to know Jesus more.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, I ask for more of You. I seek Your face. I knock on the door of deeper relationship. Thank You for Your promise to answer.
